_doc: Country-agnostic emissions->deaths dose-response for the health channel. See docs/design/emissions-to-health-dose-response.md.
_method: deaths_change = total_ambient_pm25_deaths[country] (GBD) * M[country] * emissions_change_fraction (CLEWS sector delta); M = sector_share * crf_elasticity

_sources.sector_share: McDuffie et al. 2021, Nature Communications 12:3594 (s41467-021-23853-y) + GBD MAPS/HEI; country-level shares in the supplementary
_sources.crf: GBD MR-BRT/IER + GEMM (Burnett et al. 2018, PNAS 115(38):9592); concave/supralinear over 15-40 ug/m3; elasticity ~0.78-0.86 in that range
_sources.exposure: GBD 2021/23, World Bank WDI EN.ATM.PM25.MC.M3 (GBD-sourced), HEI State of Global Air

global_ambient_sector_share_of_mortality._note: global averages; use the country value where published, else these as a documented proxy

Philippines.status: energy share = GLOBAL anchor (10.2%); PHL-specific PENDING extraction from McDuffie supplementary

South Africa.status: energy-coal share PUBLISHED (20.5%, largest single source); CRF elasticity approx at 36 ug/m3

Indonesia.status: residential dominates (~33%); energy share PENDING from McDuffie supplementary

Ethiopia.status: exposure 17-50 by data vintage; energy share PENDING from McDuffie supplementary

_crf_elasticity_note: Per-country elasticities are approximated from the concave CRF at each baseline exposure (higher exposure -> lower marginal elasticity). Recompute from GEMM/IER parameters when precision matters; the sector SHARE is the CRF-robust term.

_future_household_air_pollution._what: SEPARATE GBD risk factor: 'Household air pollution from solid fuels' (INDOOR exposure). DISTINCT from ambient PM2.5 above.
_future_household_air_pollution._for: a future household-fuel-change channel (biomass/charcoal cooking -> clean fuels/electric)
_future_household_air_pollution._use: GBD HAP rei attributable deaths (a separate IHME export; our current GBD file is AMBIENT only) + % population using solid fuels + the HAP CRF -- NOT the ambient residential share.
_future_household_air_pollution._why_distinct: McDuffie 'residential' = residential combustion's contribution to OUTDOOR ambient PM2.5; HAP = INDOOR exposure. Largest burden in low-income / high-solid-fuel-use settings (Ethiopia, rural Indonesia/Philippines).
